title: Failed to start a x86 podman machine (5.1.2) on Apple MacOS M1 14.5 with applehv
body:
### Issue Description

I tried to create a podman machine (x86) on Apple MacOS 14.5 M1 but failed. I need to run a x86 machine sometimes since I need to work on x86 for some work.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run `podman machine init --image-path https://builds.coreos.fedoraproject.org/prod/streams/stable/builds/39.20240407.3.0/x86_64/fedora-coreos-39.20240407.3.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz intel`
2. Run `podman machine start intel`


### Describe the results you received

See the error `Starting machine "intel" Error: vfkit exited unexpectedly with exit code 0`

### Describe the results you expected

The X86 machine should be started

### podman info output

```yaml
podman info                       
OS: darwin/arm64
provider: applehv
version: 5.1.2

vfkit --version
vfkit version: 0.5.1

I have Rosetta 2 installed.
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

vfkit --version
vfkit version: 0.5.1

podman --version
podman version 5.1.2

comments.body:
You need to use Podman 4.9 with QEMU for this, unless running the x86 container* with Rosetta is not enough...

\* on an arm machine

https://blog.podman.io/2024/03/migration-of-podman-4-to-podman-5-machines/

EDIT: Actually it doesn't work in 4.9 either, the qemu arch and cpu flags are hardcoded to the native architecture.

```
Error: qemu exited unexpectedly with exit code 1, stderr: qemu-system-x86_64: invalid accelerator hvf
qemu-system-x86_64: falling back to tcg
qemu-system-x86_64: unable to find CPU model 'host'

```

----

It does start when patched, but you would have to turn off the regular podman machine first so not convenient...

"Error: cannot start VM intel. VM podman-machine-qemu is currently running or starting: only one VM can be active at a time"

It would also mean that you would have to run podman-amd64 to start amd64, and podman-arm64 otherwise.

```diff
 func (v *MachineVM) addArchOptions(_ *setNewMachineCMDOpts) []string {
-       opts := []string{"-machine", "q35,accel=hvf:tcg", "-cpu", "host"}
+       opts := []string{"-machine", "q35,accel=tcg"}
        return opts
```

podman-amd64: Mach-O 64-bit executable x86_64
podman-arm64: Mach-O 64-bit executable arm64

Maybe it needs a better error message.

* https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22130

We do not support emulating the entire machine OS and I don't think we have plans to do so.
@baude @ashley-cui right?
Correct this is a non goal.
